2605.04180.title: MedFabric and EtHER: A Data-Centric Framework for Word-Level Fabrication Generation and Detection in Medical LLMs
2605.04180.paper_summary: Large Language Models exhibit strong reasoning and semantic understanding capabilities but often hallucinate in domains that require expert knowledge, among which fabrications, the generation of factually incorrect yet fluent statements, pose the greatest risk in medical contexts. Existing medical hallucination datasets inadequately capture fabrication phenomena due to limited fabrication coverage, stylistic disparities between human and LLM-authored texts, and distributional drift during hallucinated sample synthesis. To address this, we propose a data-centric pipeline to generate realistic and word-level fabrications that preserve syntactic and stylistic fidelity while introducing subtle factual deviations, resulting in MedFabric. Building upon this dataset, we introduce ETHER, a modular word-level fabrication detector integrating Text2Table Decomposition, Word Masking and Filling and Hybrid Sentence Pair Evaluation to enhance factual alignment. Empirical results demonstrate that MedFabric outperforms state-of-the-art detectors by over 15% on word-level fabrication benchmarks while maintaining consistent performance across structural similarities, offering a comprehensive framework for reliable and domain-specific factuality detection.
2605.04180.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型展現出強大的推理和語義理解能力，但在需要專業知識的領域中，經常會出現幻覺，其中虛構，即生成事實上不正確但流暢的陳述，在醫療背景中構成最大的風險。現有的醫療幻覺數據集因為虛構現象的覆蓋範圍有限、人類與大型語言模型創作文本之間的風格差異，以及在幻覺樣本合成過程中的分佈漂移，而未能充分捕捉虛構現象。為了解決這個問題，我們提出了一個以數據為中心的流程，生成保留語法和風格忠實度的現實且逐字的虛構，同時引入微妙的事實偏差，最終形成MedFabric。在此數據集的基礎上，我們介紹了ETHER，一個模塊化的逐字虛構檢測器，整合了Text2Table分解、單詞遮罩和填充以及混合句子對評估，以增強事實對齊。實證結果顯示，MedFabric在逐字虛構基準上超越了最先進的檢測器超過15%，同時在結構相似性方面保持一致的表現，提供了一個可靠且特定領域的事實性檢測的綜合框架。
2605.04180.author: Tung Sum Thomas Kwok et.al.
2605.04180.authors: Tung Sum Thomas Kwok, Qian Qian, Xiaofeng Lin, Dongxu Zhang, Jun Han, Zhichao Yang, Davin Hill, Tamer Soliman, Sanjit Singh Batra, Robert Tillman, Guang Cheng
